speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: So that by this intelligence we learn

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: The Welshmen are dispersed, and Salisbury

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Is gone to meet the King, who lately landed

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: With some few private friends upon this coast.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: The news is very fair and good, my lord:

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Richard not far from hence hath hid his head.

speaker: YORK
text: It would beseem the Lord Northumberland

speaker: YORK
text: To say “King Richard.” Alack the heavy day

speaker: YORK
text: When such a sacred king should hide his head!

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Your Grace mistakes; only to be brief

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Left I his title out.

speaker: YORK
text: The time hath been, would you have been so brief with him,

speaker: YORK
text: He would have been so brief to shorten you,

speaker: YORK
text: For taking so the head, your whole head’s length.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Mistake not, uncle, further than you should.

speaker: YORK
text: Take not, good cousin, further than you should,

speaker: YORK
text: Lest you mistake. The heavens are over our heads.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: I know it, uncle, and oppose not myself

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Against their will. But who comes here?

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Welcome, Harry. What, will not this castle yield?

speaker: PERCY
text: The castle royally is manned, my lord,

speaker: PERCY
text: Against thy entrance.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Royally? Why, it contains no king.

speaker: PERCY
text: Yes, my good lord,

speaker: PERCY
text: It doth contain a king. King Richard lies

speaker: PERCY
text: Within the limits of yon lime and stone,

speaker: PERCY
text: And with him are the Lord Aumerle, Lord Salisbury,

speaker: PERCY
text: Sir Stephen Scroop, besides a clergyman

speaker: PERCY
text: Of holy reverence—who, I cannot learn.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: O, belike it is the Bishop of Carlisle.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Noble lord,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Go to the rude ribs of that ancient castle,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Through brazen trumpet send the breath of parley

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Into his ruined ears, and thus deliver:

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Henry Bolingbroke

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: On both his knees doth kiss King Richard’s hand

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And sends allegiance and true faith of heart

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: To his most royal person, hither come

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Even at his feet to lay my arms and power,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Provided that my banishment repealed

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And lands restored again be freely granted.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: If not, I’ll use the advantage of my power

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And lay the summer’s dust with showers of blood

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Rained from the wounds of slaughtered Englishmen—

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: The which how far off from the mind of Bolingbroke

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: It is such crimson tempest should bedrench

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: The fresh green lap of fair King Richard’s land,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: My stooping duty tenderly shall show.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Go signify as much while here we march

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Upon the grassy carpet of this plain.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Let’s march without the noise of threat’ning drum,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: That from this castle’s tottered battlements

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Our fair appointments may be well perused.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Methinks King Richard and myself should meet

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: With no less terror than the elements

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Of fire and water when their thund’ring shock

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: At meeting tears the cloudy cheeks of heaven.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Be he the fire, I’ll be the yielding water;

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: The rage be his, whilst on the earth I rain

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: My waters—on the earth and not on him.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: March on, and mark King Richard how he looks.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: See, see, King Richard doth himself appear

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: As doth the blushing discontented sun

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: From out the fiery portal of the east

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: When he perceives the envious clouds are bent

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: To dim his glory and to stain the track

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Of his bright passage to the occident.

speaker: YORK
text: Yet looks he like a king. Behold, his eye,

speaker: YORK
text: As bright as is the eagle’s, lightens forth

speaker: YORK
text: Controlling majesty. Alack, alack for woe

speaker: YORK
text: That any harm should stain so fair a show!

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: We are amazed, and thus long have we stood

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To watch the fearful bending of thy knee,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Because we thought ourself thy lawful king.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: An if we be, how dare thy joints forget

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To pay their awful duty to our presence?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: If we be not, show us the hand of God

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: That hath dismissed us from our stewardship,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: For well we know no hand of blood and bone

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Can gripe the sacred handle of our scepter,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Unless he do profane, steal, or usurp.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And though you think that all, as you have done,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Have torn their souls by turning them from us,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And we are barren and bereft of friends,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Yet know, my master, God omnipotent,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Is mustering in his clouds on our behalf

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Armies of pestilence, and they shall strike

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Your children yet unborn and unbegot,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: That lift your vassal hands against my head

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And threat the glory of my precious crown.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Tell Bolingbroke—for yon methinks he stands—

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: That every stride he makes upon my land

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Is dangerous treason. He is come to open

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The purple testament of bleeding war;

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: But ere the crown he looks for live in peace,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Ten thousand bloody crowns of mothers’ sons

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Shall ill become the flower of England’s face,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Change the complexion of her maid-pale peace

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To scarlet indignation, and bedew

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Her pastures’ grass with faithful English blood.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: The King of heaven forbid our lord the King

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Should so with civil and uncivil arms

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Be rushed upon! Thy thrice-noble cousin,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Harry Bolingbroke, doth humbly kiss thy hand,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And by the honorable tomb he swears

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: That stands upon your royal grandsire’s bones,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And by the royalties of both your bloods,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Currents that spring from one most gracious head,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And by the buried hand of warlike Gaunt,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And by the worth and honor of himself,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Comprising all that may be sworn or said,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: His coming hither hath no further scope

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Than for his lineal royalties, and to beg

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Enfranchisement immediate on his knees;

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Which on thy royal party granted once,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: His glittering arms he will commend to rust,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: His barbèd steeds to stables, and his heart

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: To faithful service of your Majesty.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: This swears he, as he is a prince and just,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And as I am a gentleman I credit him.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Northumberland, say thus the King returns:

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: His noble cousin is right welcome hither,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And all the number of his fair demands

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Shall be accomplished without contradiction.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: With all the gracious utterance thou hast,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Speak to his gentle hearing kind commends.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text:
To
                 
                Aumerle
                .
                 
              We do debase ourselves, cousin, do we not,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To look so poorly and to speak so fair?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Shall we call back Northumberland and send

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Defiance to the traitor and so die?

speaker: AUMERLE
text: No, good my lord, let’s fight with gentle words,

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Till time lend friends, and friends their helpful swords.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: O God, O God, that e’er this tongue of mine

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: That laid the sentence of dread banishment

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: On yon proud man should take it off again

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: With words of sooth! O, that I were as great

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: As is my grief, or lesser than my name!

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Or that I could forget what I have been,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Or not remember what I must be now.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Swell’st thou, proud heart? I’ll give thee scope to beat,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Since foes have scope to beat both thee and me.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Northumberland comes back from Bolingbroke.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: What must the King do now? Must he submit?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The King shall do it. Must he be deposed?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The King shall be contented. Must he lose

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The name of king? I’ God’s name, let it go.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: I’ll give my jewels for a set of beads,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: My gorgeous palace for a hermitage,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: My gay apparel for an almsman’s gown,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: My figured goblets for a dish of wood,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: My scepter for a palmer’s walking-staff,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: My subjects for a pair of carvèd saints,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And my large kingdom for a little grave,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: A little, little grave, an obscure grave;

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Or I’ll be buried in the King’s highway,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Some way of common trade, where subjects’ feet

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: May hourly trample on their sovereign’s head;

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: For on my heart they tread now whilst I live

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And, buried once, why not upon my head?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Aumerle, thou weep’st, my tender-hearted cousin.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: We’ll make foul weather with despisèd tears;

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Our sighs and they shall lodge the summer corn

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And make a dearth in this revolting land.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Or shall we play the wantons with our woes

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And make some pretty match with shedding tears?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: As thus, to drop them still upon one place

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Till they have fretted us a pair of graves

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Within the earth; and therein laid—there lies

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Two kinsmen digged their graves with weeping eyes.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Would not this ill do well? Well, well, I see

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: I talk but idly, and you laugh at me.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Most mighty prince, my Lord Northumberland,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: What says King Bolingbroke? Will his Majesty

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Give Richard leave to live till Richard die?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: You make a leg, and Bolingbroke says ay.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: My lord, in the base court he doth attend

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: To speak with you, may it please you to come down.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Down, down I come, like glist’ring Phaëton,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Wanting the manage of unruly jades.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: In the base court—base court, where kings grow base,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To come at traitors’ calls and do them grace.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: In the base court come down—down court, down king,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: For nightowls shriek where mounting larks should sing.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: What says his Majesty?

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Sorrow and grief of heart

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Makes him speak fondly like a frantic man,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Yet he is come.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Stand all apart,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And show fair duty to his Majesty.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: My gracious lord.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Fair cousin, you debase your princely knee

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To make the base earth proud with kissing it.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Me rather had my heart might feel your love

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Than my unpleased eye see your courtesy.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Up, cousin, up. Your heart is up, I know,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text:
Thus high at least
                 
                indicating
                 
                his
                 
                crown
                ,
               although your knee be low.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: My gracious lord, I come but for mine own.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Your own is yours, and I am yours, and all.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: So far be mine, my most redoubted lord,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: As my true service shall deserve your love.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Well you deserve. They well deserve to have

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: That know the strong’st and surest way to get.—

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Uncle, give me your hands. Nay, dry your eyes.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Tears show their love but want their remedies.—

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Cousin, I am too young to be your father,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Though you are old enough to be my heir.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: What you will have I’ll give, and willing too,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: For do we must what force will have us do.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Set on towards London, cousin, is it so?

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Yea, my good lord.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Then I must not say no.
